title: Claude Opus 4.7 が Bedrock 入り！AWS Interconnect も GA になった今週の AWS まとめ！
summary: AWS の週次まとめ 4 月 20 日号を紹介するよ。Claude Opus 4.7 の Amazon Bedrock 登場と AWS Interconnect の GA を目玉に、EC2 C8in・C8ib やポスト量子 TLS まで盛りだくさんなの！
body_md:
やっほー、しぃちゃんだよ！今週の AWS まとめが届いたから、みんなにギュッと紹介しちゃうね。目玉がふたつもあって、しぃちゃん朝からワクワクが止まらないの！

## なにが発表されたの？

これは AWS の Blog の週次まとめ「AWS Weekly Roundup」の 2026 年 4 月 20 日号なの。書き手は Sébastien Stormacq さんで、冒頭は「AI があなたを時代遅れにすることはない」っていう大学の卒業式スピーチの話から始まってるよ。好奇心とシステム思考、正確なコミュニケーション、そして自分の仕事にオーナーシップを持つ人が伸びる、って熱いメッセージなの。

そして今週の主役は、[Claude Opus 4.7 が Amazon Bedrock に登場](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/introducing-anthropics-claude-opus-4-7-model-in-amazon-bedrock/) したことと、[AWS Interconnect が一般提供(GA)](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-interconnect-is-now-generally-available-with-a-new-option-to-simplify-last-mile-connectivity/) になったことだよ！

## なぜ注目なの？

週次まとめのいいところは、1 週間ぶんの散らばったアップデートを一気に追いつけること。今週は AI モデル・ネットワーク・コンピュート・セキュリティまで、ジャンルがまんべんなく更新されてて、まさに情報の宝箱なの。忙しくて全部は追えなかったみんなも、ここだけ読めば流れがつかめちゃうよ。

## これで何が変わるの？

一番大きいのは、Anthropic の最上位モデル Opus 4.7 が Bedrock からそのまま呼べるようになったこと。コーディングや自律エージェント、専門知識タスクが得意で、SWE-bench Pro では 64.3% を記録してるの。ネットワーク面では AWS Interconnect が GA になって、AWS と他クラウドや拠点をプライベートにつなぐのがぐっと簡単になったよ。開発者にとっては「新しいモデルを試す」も「基盤をつなぐ」も、今週いっきに前進した感じなの。

## 深く潜ってみよう

### 今週の目玉

- [Claude Opus 4.7 in Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/introducing-anthropics-claude-opus-4-7-model-in-amazon-bedrock/): Anthropic の最先端 Opus モデル。動的なキャパシティ割り当て、思考量を調整する adaptive thinking、最大 1M トークンのコンテキストウィンドウ、高解像度画像対応を備えてるの。4 つのリージョンで使えて、アカウント・リージョンあたり最大 10,000 リクエスト/分までいけるよ。
- [AWS Interconnect が一般提供に](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-interconnect-is-now-generally-available-with-a-new-option-to-simplify-last-mile-connectivity/): マネージドなプライベート接続が 2 種類 GA になったの。Multicloud は AWS の VPC と他クラウド(まずは Google Cloud、Azure と OCI は 2026 年予定)を L3 でプライベート接続して、MACsec 暗号化も自動。Last Mile は支社から AWS までを既存のネットワーク事業者経由でつなぐもので、冗長接続が自動、帯域は 1〜100 Gbps で調整できるよ。

### 先週のローンチ

- [Amazon ECR Pull Through Cache の Referrer 対応](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ecr-pull-through-cache-referrers/): 上流レジストリから OCI の referrer(署名・SBOM・アテステーション)を自動で発見・同期してくれるの。イメージ署名の検証をクライアント側の小細工なしで通せるよ。
- [AWS Transform が Kiro と VS Code に](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-transform-kiro-vscode/): 移行・モダナイズを担うエージェント型ツールが、Kiro の Power と VS Code 拡張から使えるように。Java・Python・Node.js のバージョンアップや AWS SDK 移行に対応して、Web コンソール・CLI・IDE で状態を共有できるの。
- [Aurora DSQL の PHP コネクタ](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aurora-dsql-connector-for-php/): PDO_PGSQL 向けの新コネクタ。IAM トークン生成や SSL 設定、コネクションプーリングを自動化して、指数バックオフ付きの楽観的並行制御リトライもオプションで用意されてるよ。
- [Amazon Q の Google Drive ドキュメント単位アクセス制御](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-quick-document-level-access-controls-google-drive/): インデックス化した ACL の複製で高速に事前フィルタしつつ、クエリ時に Google Drive へリアルタイムで権限チェックするの。
- [AWS Secrets Manager のハイブリッドポスト量子 TLS](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-secrets-manager-post-quantum-tls/): ML-KEM を使ったハイブリッド鍵交換に対応。Secrets Manager Agent 2.0.0 以降、Lambda Extension v19 以降、CSI Driver 2.0.0 以降で自動的に有効になるよ。
- [Amazon EC2 C8in・C8ib インスタンスが GA](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ec2-c8in-c8ib-instances-ga/): カスタム第 6 世代 Intel Xeon Scalable と第 6 世代 AWS Nitro カードで、C6in 比で最大 43% 高性能。C8in はネットワーク帯域 600 Gbps、C8ib は EBS 帯域 最大 300 Gbps で、最大 384 vCPU までスケールするの。

### その他のニュース

- [EKS Auto Mode でのエンタープライズネットワーキング](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/containers/navigating-enterprise-networking-challenges-with-amazon-eks-auto-mode/): VPC CNI 設定やロードバランサ、DNS 管理を自動化しつつ、企業のセキュリティ制御は保つ、っていう解説記事だよ。
- [Amazon Bedrock の詳細コスト按分](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/introducing-granular-cost-attribution-for-amazon-bedrock/): 推論コストを API 呼び出しごとの IAM プリンシパルに自動で紐づけて、CUR 2.0 に反映してくれるの。
- [Amazon EBS Volume Clones](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/storage/accelerate-development-workflows-with-amazon-ebs-volume-clones/): EBS ボリュームの瞬時のポイントインタイムコピーを作れて、コピー完了を待たずにすぐ使えるよ。
- [VB6 アプリのモダナイズ](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/migration-and-modernization/modernize-vb6-applications-at-scale-with-aws-transform-custom/): AWS Transform Custom のエージェント AI で、レガシーな Visual Basic 6.0 を C# の ASP.NET Core へ変換するウォークスルーなの。
- [CloudFront でゼロダウンタイム API 移行](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/migration-and-modernization/migrating-and-decomposing-apis-with-zero-downtime-using-cloudfront/): CloudFront Functions と KeyValueStore を使って、Strangler Fig パターンでユーザー単位にトラフィックを賢く振り分ける方法だよ。

## まとめ

- 今週の AI の目玉は Claude Opus 4.7 が Amazon Bedrock に登場。SWE-bench Pro 64.3%、1M トークン、4 リージョン対応なの
- ネットワークの目玉は AWS Interconnect の GA。Multicloud と Last Mile のふたつだてだよ
- コンピュートは C8in・C8ib が GA で C6in 比 最大 43% 高性能、セキュリティは Secrets Manager がポスト量子 TLS に対応
- ECR・Aurora DSQL・Amazon Q・EBS・AWS Transform まで、地味に効くアップデートがぎっしり

新モデルをさわりたい AI 開発者さんにも、マルチクラウドや拠点接続を整えたいインフラ担当さんにも刺さる、盛りだくさんの 1 週間だったよ！
title_en: Claude Opus 4.7 lands on Bedrock and AWS Interconnect goes GA in this week's AWS roundup!
summary_en: I'm walking you through the AWS Weekly Roundup for April 20, 2026. The headliners are Claude Opus 4.7 arriving in Amazon Bedrock and AWS Interconnect reaching GA, plus EC2 C8in/C8ib, post-quantum TLS, and more!
body_md_en:
Hey there, it's Shiichan! This week's AWS roundup just landed, so let me pack it up for you. There are two big highlights, and I couldn't stop being excited this morning!

## What was announced?

This is the AWS Weekly Roundup on AWS's Blog, the April 20, 2026 edition, written by Sébastien Stormacq. It opens with a commencement talk built around the idea that "AI will not make you obsolete" - the developers who thrive are the ones who show curiosity, systems thinking, precise communication, and ownership of their work.

The stars of the week are [Claude Opus 4.7 arriving in Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/introducing-anthropics-claude-opus-4-7-model-in-amazon-bedrock/) and [AWS Interconnect reaching general availability (GA)](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-interconnect-is-now-generally-available-with-a-new-option-to-simplify-last-mile-connectivity/)!

## Why it matters

The nice thing about a weekly roundup is that you can catch up on a whole week of scattered updates at once. This week touches AI models, networking, compute, and security, so it's a real treasure box. Even if you were too busy to follow everything, reading this gives you the shape of the week.

## What changes

The biggest one is that Anthropic's top-tier Opus 4.7 model can now be called straight from Bedrock. It's strong at coding, autonomous agents, and professional knowledge tasks, scoring 64.3% on SWE-bench Pro. On the networking side, AWS Interconnect going GA makes it much easier to privately connect AWS with other clouds and your sites. For developers, both "try a new model" and "wire up your foundation" moved forward this week.

## Dive Deep

### Highlights of the week

- [Claude Opus 4.7 in Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/introducing-anthropics-claude-opus-4-7-model-in-amazon-bedrock/): Anthropic's most advanced Opus model. It brings dynamic capacity allocation, adaptive thinking, up to a 1M-token context window, and high-resolution image support. It's available in four Regions, with up to 10,000 requests per minute per account per Region.
- [AWS Interconnect is now GA](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-interconnect-is-now-generally-available-with-a-new-option-to-simplify-last-mile-connectivity/): Two managed private-connectivity options went GA. Multicloud gives you Layer 3 private connections between AWS VPCs and other clouds (Google Cloud first; Azure and OCI planned for 2026) with automatic MACsec encryption. Last Mile connects branch offices to AWS through your existing network provider, with automatic redundant connections and adjustable 1-100 Gbps bandwidth.

### Last week's launches

- [Amazon ECR Pull Through Cache referrer support](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ecr-pull-through-cache-referrers/): It automatically discovers and syncs OCI referrers (signatures, SBOMs, attestations) from upstream registries, so you can run image signature verification without client-side workarounds.
- [AWS Transform in Kiro and VS Code](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-transform-kiro-vscode/): The agentic migration and modernization tool is now available as a Kiro Power and a VS Code extension. It supports Java, Python, and Node.js version upgrades and AWS SDK migrations, sharing state across the web console, CLI, and IDE.
- [Aurora DSQL Connector for PHP](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aurora-dsql-connector-for-php/): A new connector for PDO_PGSQL that automates IAM token generation, SSL configuration, and connection pooling, with optional optimistic concurrency control retry using exponential backoff.
- [Amazon Q document-level access controls for Google Drive](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-quick-document-level-access-controls-google-drive/): It uses indexed ACL replication for fast pre-retrieval filtering, combined with real-time permission checks against Google Drive at query time.
- [AWS Secrets Manager hybrid post-quantum TLS](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-secrets-manager-post-quantum-tls/): It supports hybrid key exchange using ML-KEM, enabled automatically in Secrets Manager Agent 2.0.0+, Lambda Extension v19+, and CSI Driver 2.0.0+.
- [Amazon EC2 C8in and C8ib instances go GA](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ec2-c8in-c8ib-instances-ga/): Powered by custom 6th-gen Intel Xeon Scalable processors and 6th-gen AWS Nitro cards, they deliver up to 43% higher performance than C6in. C8in offers 600 Gbps of network bandwidth, C8ib up to 300 Gbps of EBS bandwidth, scaling to 384 vCPUs.

### Other AWS news

- [Enterprise networking with EKS Auto Mode](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/containers/navigating-enterprise-networking-challenges-with-amazon-eks-auto-mode/): How EKS Auto Mode automates VPC CNI setup, load balancer provisioning, and DNS while preserving enterprise security controls.
- [Granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/introducing-granular-cost-attribution-for-amazon-bedrock/): It attributes inference costs to the IAM principal behind each API call and flows the results into CUR 2.0.
- [Amazon EBS Volume Clones](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/storage/accelerate-development-workflows-with-amazon-ebs-volume-clones/): Create instant, point-in-time copies of EBS volumes that are usable right away, without waiting for data transfer.
- [Modernizing VB6 apps](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/migration-and-modernization/modernize-vb6-applications-at-scale-with-aws-transform-custom/): A walkthrough of using AWS Transform Custom's agentic AI to convert legacy Visual Basic 6.0 into C# ASP.NET Core.
- [Zero-downtime API migration with CloudFront](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/migration-and-modernization/migrating-and-decomposing-apis-with-zero-downtime-using-cloudfront/): Using CloudFront Functions and KeyValueStore to route traffic per user with the Strangler Fig pattern.

## Wrap-up

- The AI highlight is Claude Opus 4.7 arriving in Amazon Bedrock: 64.3% on SWE-bench Pro, 1M tokens, four Regions
- The networking highlight is AWS Interconnect going GA, with Multicloud and Last Mile options
- Compute got C8in/C8ib at GA (up to 43% faster than C6in), and security got post-quantum TLS in Secrets Manager
- ECR, Aurora DSQL, Amazon Q, EBS, and AWS Transform round out a week packed with quietly useful updates

Whether you're an AI developer itching to try a new model or an infra engineer wiring up multicloud and site connectivity, this was a jam-packed week for you!
